content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic reward mechanisms (from autonomous agents, Topic A) can be repurposed to prioritize what new information an active learning agent retains in its long-term memory, solving the 2024-2025 SOTA challenge of catastrophic forgetting and uninformative memory bloat in open-ended active learning agents (Topic B). This creates a "curiosity gatekeeper" memory architecture where only surprising, unpredicted inputs that trigger intrinsic curiosity are encoded into long-term memory, rather than storing all encountered data.
